acceptance_targets: [CH13-F001] PASS if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:395, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:457, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:479 contain no more than two Ravi contact attempts and each attempt introduces new leverage; FAIL if three or more beats repeat the same ask with no new consequence.

[CH13-F002] PASS if the revised section around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:333 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:359 includes at least one concrete physiological aftermath beat linked to the confrontation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173, and comic relief does not appear before boundary-setting is locked; FAIL if the scene jumps straight from escape to logistics/jokes without embodied consequence.

[CH13-F003] PASS if Ravi's revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173 contain at least two broken starts or fragments plus one clear pivot from control to regret and back; FAIL if the sequence remains all clean declaratives.

[CH13-F004] PASS if Anika's revised message at the snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:411 equivalent uses child-specific concrete stakes and differs in syntax/rhythm from Priya's message at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:389; FAIL if both messages read like the same boundary template spoken by different names.

[CH13-F006] PASS if each revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:427, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:517 grounds the idea in at least one concrete sensory or physical action cue; FAIL if the passage still relies on standalone abstract claim language.

[FBR-003] PASS if ankle laterality is internally consistent after revision: either left-ankle marker remains consistent, or a new right-ankle condition is explicitly introduced and carried forward. FAIL if a one-off right-ankle mention remains unsupported.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:395, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:457, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:479 contain no more than two Ravi contact attempts and each attempt introduces new leverage; FAIL if three or more beats repeat the same ask with no new consequence.
findings.problem: After the rupture, Ravi contact beats recur in similar shape (call/text/voicemail/call) without enough leverage change, so the back half repeats pressure rather than escalating it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or differentiate the contact sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:395, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:457, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:479 so each remaining beat changes power (e.g., procedural claim -> emotional plea -> explicit consequence) instead of restating 'come back/reset.'

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised section around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:333 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:359 includes at least one concrete physiological aftermath beat linked to the confrontation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173, and comic relief does not appear before boundary-setting is locked; FAIL if the scene jumps straight from escape to logistics/jokes without embodied consequence.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: after a coercive flashpoint, the chapter shifts quickly into procedural logistics and humor, which softens the immediate emotional/physical aftermath and defuses narrative tension too early.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:333 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:359, add embodied aftermath tied to the doorway conflict at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173 before moving to practical planning; move or trim early comic relief at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:473 so the rupture cost stays on-page longer.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Ravi's revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173 contain at least two broken starts or fragments plus one clear pivot from control to regret and back; FAIL if the sequence remains all clean declaratives.
findings.problem: Ravi's peak-pressure speech stays syntactically polished and fully controlled when his style profile calls for control failure patterns (fragments, late apology pivots, destabilized command voice).
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Ravi's confrontation lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:149, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:173 to include interrupted phrasing, one self-correction, and a late command/apology oscillation that sounds like panic leaking through procedural language.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Anika's revised message at the snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:411 equivalent uses child-specific concrete stakes and differs in syntax/rhythm from Priya's message at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:389; FAIL if both messages read like the same boundary template spoken by different names.
findings.problem: Anika's boundary language in late-chapter messaging converges toward Priya's mediator register, reducing idiolect separation between a paralegal adult and an eleven-year-old focal character.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep Priya's formal boundary script at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:389, but recast Anika's reply at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:411 in her own concrete stress register (logistics/body/school stakes) rather than mirrored legal framing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two instances of the same 'smelled like X and Y and Z' construction remain, including the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:335; FAIL if three or more remain.
findings.problem: Recurring sensory sentence template ('smelled like X and Y and Z') appears at least three times, reading as a prose tic rather than fresh scene perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the sensory lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:335 so at least one instance shifts to a different construction (action-triggered smell, contrastive image, or character-filtered sensory reaction).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:427, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:517 grounds the idea in at least one concrete sensory or physical action cue; FAIL if the passage still relies on standalone abstract claim language.
findings.problem: Several aphoristic abstractions drift away from Anika's concrete lexical signature, flattening immediacy at key emotional turns.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:427, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:517, replace thesis-like phrasing with embodied details (object handling, bodily cue, money/logistics action) that carry the same meaning through scene evidence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if ankle laterality is internally consistent after revision: either left-ankle marker remains consistent, or a new right-ankle condition is explicitly introduced and carried forward. FAIL if a one-off right-ankle mention remains unsupported.
findings.problem: Ravi's chronic marker is established as left-ankle stiffness throughout the book, but Chapter 13 suddenly says he is rolling his right ankle. This is a cross-chapter physical-state contradiction with no setup or follow-through.
findings.rewrite_direction: Normalize laterality at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4626 to match established continuity (left ankle), or explicitly introduce a new right-ankle issue with causal setup and downstream references in snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4622-4700 and later continuity beats (e.g., snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5165, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6520).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
